channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Across from the junction with Hatherley Court Road and Bournside Road, 22nd June
item.description:
Change in Road surface levels as recent tarmac has gone and it is a trip hazard for anyone crossing the road here.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Hatherley Court Road, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sunken metal utility cover creating a trip hazard on a busy footpath, 21st June
item.description:
The sunken metal utility cover is located on the pavement approximately outside 104 Farmfield Road. The cover has dropped below the surrounding pavement level, creating an uneven surface and a clear trip hazard for pedestrians. This footpath is well used by local residents, including people walking to nearby amenities, and the defect presents a particular risk to elderly pedestrians, people with mobility issues and anyone pushing a pushchair. The surrounding surface is also beginning to show signs of deterioration around the cover. I would be grateful if this could be inspected and repaired before the defect worsens further.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 102, Farmfield Road, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wide pothole on Doverdale Drive Longlevens, 21st June
item.description:
Outside 18 and 19 Doverdale Drive Longlevens GL2 0NN

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Doverdale Drive, Longlevens, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Whole road is dangerously, 21st June
item.description:
All along gotherington road, all of it. Embarrassing.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cleeve Road, Gotherington, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Residential Hedge, OVERGROWING INTO THE PEDESTRIAN HIGHWAY PAVEMENT/ RIGHT NEXT TO THE BUSY PEDESTRIAN CROSSING, 21st June
item.description:
Contractors have attended the northernmost access to the pedestrian crossing BUT FAILED TO EVEN ATTEMPT THE SIGNIFICANTLY OVERGROWN RESIDENTIAL HEDGE. SUGGEST THAT THE RESIDENT BE VISITED PROFESSIONALLY< AND INSTRUCTED TO ACT IMMEDIATELY. NO BIRDS NESTS ARE INVOLVED.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cheltenham Road, Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Ash Tree located on private land

item.title: There is no pathway turning into stanley place and the hedge on the corner stops people turning in from seeing school children, push chairs or wheelchairs. I have lived at this address since February and seen too many near accidents of children walking to and from school. I think my street needs a safe pathway or clear signs for the blind corner for the puplic to use as stanley place is a shortcut through to coronation square and is used daily by many., 21st June
item.description:
Entering stanley place there is no safe path.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Stanley Place, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 21st June
item.description:
The constant stream of dumped rubbish continues in this location. Why does the council allow this to happen time and time again? This is the 4th fly tip this week!!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 17, Alstone Lane, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

pothole - 45 cm wide deeper than tennis ball no damage to vehicle

item.title: Public Footpath Unusable, 21st June
item.description:

item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Flytipping, 21st June
item.description:
Bags of waste Mattress Carpet. Time to install cameras and make the residents take their rubbish to the tip like everyone else?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cotswold Road, Lynworth, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Public footpath blocked off, 20th June
item.description:
There is a barrier blocking the public footpath (presumably placed there by the landowner). Behind the barrier the path is heavily overgrown and almost completely impassable.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Timbercombe Lane, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead treee, 20th June
item.description:
Tree is dead and Is a hazard in strong winds to property
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dunstan Glen, Churchdown, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Neglected area, 20th June
item.description:
The whole area is neglected tree limbs are hanging down to ground level growing out from the bottom of trees. Drains around 18 bedford avenue before and after are nearly closed. A drain outside the school gate is broken and cars parking on pavements at the T junction of Bedford avenue and alstone Lane is making it dangerous to come out of Bedford avenue onto alstone Lane.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Bedford Avenue, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loose drain cover is keeping us awake at night. This happens every few months. Can you please find a permanent solution. Thank you., 20th June
item.description:
Opposite the main entrance to Spirax Sarco HQ by the traffic island near the wall to Langton Place.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Langton Place, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 13 Insley Gardens, 20th June
item.description:
Car has been say stationary unmoved now for nearly 6 weeks, parked away from the curb and is causing issues with access
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Insley Gardens, Hucclecote, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet
